BSTD
A lightweight C library providing common data structures and utilities for systems programming.

String Views

Non-owning string slices. More...

bstd_strview bstd_strview_new (const char *cstr)
 Creates a string view from a null-terminated C string.

 Prints a string view followed by a newline.

 Prints a string view without a trailing newline.

 Checks whether a string view starts with a prefix.

 Checks whether a string view ends with a suffix.

 Checks whether a string view contains another view.

 Compares two string views for equality.

 Consumes the next character from a view.

 Advances a string view by a number of characters.

 Consumes a prefix if present.
 
bool bstd_strview_is_empty (bstd_strview s)
 Checks whether a string view is empty.

 Removes leading whitespace from a view.
 
void bstd_strview_trim_right (bstd_strview *s)
 Removes trailing whitespace from a view.

 Removes leading and trailing whitespace.

 Creates a line iterator.

 Returns the next segment from a split iterator.
 

Detailed Description

Non-owning string slices.

Macro Definition Documentation

◆ BSTD_STRVIEW_EMPTY

#define BSTD_STRVIEW_EMPTY
Value:
(bstd_strview) \
{ \
.data = NULL, .length = 0 \
}
bstd_strview
Definition strview.h:15

Function Documentation

◆ bstd_strview_advance()

bool bstd_strview_advance ( bstd_strview *  s,
size_t  n 
)

Advances a string view by a number of characters.

Moves the beginning of the view forward by n characters.

Parameters
sString view to modify.
nNumber of characters to skip.
Returns
true on success, false if the operation would move beyond the end of the view.

◆ bstd_strview_consume_prefix()

bool bstd_strview_consume_prefix ( bstd_strview *  s,
bstd_strview  prefix 
)

Consumes a prefix if present.

If the string view begins with the specified prefix, the prefix is removed from the view.

Parameters
sString view to modify.
prefixPrefix to consume.
Returns
true if the prefix was consumed, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_ends_with()

bool bstd_strview_ends_with ( bstd_strview  s,
bstd_strview  sufix 
)

Checks whether a string view ends with a suffix.

Parameters
sSource string view.
sufixSuffix to compare.
Returns
true if the source ends with the suffix, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_equals()

bool bstd_strview_equals ( bstd_strview  s,
bstd_strview  c 
)

Compares two string views for equality.

Both length and contents must match.

Parameters
sFirst string view.
cSecond string view.
Returns
true if both views contain identical data, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_includes()

bool bstd_strview_includes ( bstd_strview  s,
bstd_strview  search 
)

Checks whether a string view contains another view.

Searches for the first occurrence of the provided substring within the source view.

Parameters
sSource string view.
searchSubstring to search for.
Returns
true if found, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_is_empty()

bool bstd_strview_is_empty ( bstd_strview  s)

Checks whether a string view is empty.

A view is considered empty if its length is zero or if it contains only whitespace characters.

Parameters
sString view to check.
Returns
true if empty, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_new()

bstd_strview bstd_strview_new ( const char *  cstr)

Creates a string view from a null-terminated C string.

Creates a non-owning view over the provided string. No memory allocation or copy is performed.

Parameters
cstrNull-terminated source string.
Returns
A string view referencing the original string.
Warning
The source string must remain valid for the lifetime of the returned view.

◆ bstd_strview_next_char()

bool bstd_strview_next_char ( bstd_strview *  s,
char *  out 
)

Consumes the next character from a view.

Writes the first character to out and advances the view by one character.

No memory allocation is performed.

Parameters
sString view to consume from.
outOutput character.
Returns
true if a character was consumed, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_print()

void bstd_strview_print ( bstd_strview  s)

Prints a string view followed by a newline.

Writes the contents of the string view to stdout.

Parameters
sString view to print.

◆ bstd_strview_print_raw()

void bstd_strview_print_raw ( bstd_strview  s)

Prints a string view without a trailing newline.

Writes the contents of the string view to stdout.

Parameters
sString view to print.

◆ bstd_strview_slice()

bstd_strview_result bstd_strview_slice ( bstd_strview  s,
size_t  start_idx,
size_t  end_idx 
)

Creates a slice of a string view.

Returns a new view referencing the range [start_idx, end_idx) of the source view.

No memory allocation or copy is performed.

Parameters
sSource string view.
start_idxStarting index (inclusive).
end_idxEnding index (exclusive).
Returns
A result containing the sliced view on success, or an out-of-bounds error.
Warning
The range must satisfy: start_idx <= end_idx <= s.length

◆ bstd_strview_split()

bstd_strview_split_iter bstd_strview_split ( bstd_strview *  s,
bstd_strview  q 
)

Creates a split iterator.

Creates an iterator that yields segments of the source view separated by the specified delimiter.

No memory allocation is performed.

Parameters
sSource string view.
qDelimiter.
Returns
A split iterator.

◆ bstd_strview_split_iter_next()

bstd_strview bstd_strview_split_iter_next ( bstd_strview_split_iter *  iter)

Returns the next segment from a split iterator.

Retrieves the next token separated by the iterator's delimiter.

Parameters
iterSplit iterator.
Returns
The next token, or BSTD_STRVIEW_EMPTY if iteration has completed.

◆ bstd_strview_split_lines()

bstd_strview_split_iter bstd_strview_split_lines ( bstd_strview *  s)

Creates a line iterator.

Equivalent to splitting on '
'.

Parameters
sSource string view.
Returns
A split iterator over lines.

◆ bstd_strview_split_once()

bstd_strview_split_once_result bstd_strview_split_once ( bstd_strview *  s,
bstd_strview  q 
)

Splits a string view at the first occurrence of a delimiter.

Searches for the first occurrence of the delimiter and returns the left and right portions.

Example:

"key=value"
left -> "key"
right -> "value"
Parameters
sSource string view.
qDelimiter.
Returns
A structure containing the left and right views around the delimiter.
Note
If the delimiter is not found, the left view contains the original string and the right view is empty.

◆ bstd_strview_starts_with()

bool bstd_strview_starts_with ( bstd_strview  s,
bstd_strview  prefix 
)

Checks whether a string view starts with a prefix.

Parameters
sSource string view.
prefixPrefix to compare.
Returns
true if the source begins with the prefix, false otherwise.

◆ bstd_strview_trim()

void bstd_strview_trim ( bstd_strview *  s)

Removes leading and trailing whitespace.

Equivalent to calling:

bstd_strview_trim_left(s);
bstd_strview_trim_right(s);
bstd_strview_trim_right
void bstd_strview_trim_right(bstd_strview *s)
Removes trailing whitespace from a view.
Definition strview.c:145
bstd_strview_trim_left
void bstd_strview_trim_left(bstd_strview *s)
Removes leading whitespace from a view.
Definition strview.c:131
Parameters
sString view to modify.

◆ bstd_strview_trim_left()

void bstd_strview_trim_left ( bstd_strview *  s)

Removes leading whitespace from a view.

Advances the beginning of the view until the first non-whitespace character is reached.

No memory allocation or copy is performed.

Parameters
sString view to modify.

◆ bstd_strview_trim_right()

void bstd_strview_trim_right ( bstd_strview *  s)

Removes trailing whitespace from a view.

Shrinks the view until the last character is not whitespace.

No memory allocation or copy is performed.

Parameters
sString view to modify.
Generated by  1.9.8
